query: I have a new product idea, but it is still rough. Interview me first so we know what should exist before anything gets built.
There is an existing workflow I want to improve. It might touch UI, data, permissions, and rollout risk. Ask questions and choose the right planning path.
Before touching code, help me understand where this behavior is owned in the repo and ask only the questions the code cannot answer.
I need a small feature plan. It should be simple, but ask the important questions before we build it.
Stress test this plan and ask the single most important unresolved question before we commit to it.
I want a clickable mock for a new flow. Include look and feel, prototype tech, fake data, and a later implementation plan.
Help me decide a UI flow, but I need to see concrete options before choosing rather than deciding from prose.
I need to choose between two approaches. Ask questions until the tradeoffs, risks, and default recommendation are clear.
We need to migrate a risky system. It touches auth, external callbacks, schema, admin operations, and rollback. Interview me before planning.
Continue the interview after compaction. The saved session state says the last question is still unanswered.
Help me map the codebase area that owns a feature. I may implement later, but right now I want evidence and the next useful question.
Use the existing project docs and decision records before asking me what is still open in this plan.
Run the test suite and fix whatever fails.
Generate a visual asset for a website hero.
Summarize this document and extract the totals into a spreadsheet.
Commit and push the current staged changes.
Install error monitoring in this web app.
Review this change for security issues only.
Make this component faster and reduce unnecessary renders.
Look up the current pricing for this external service and tell me which plan fits.
Create a spreadsheet with sample monthly data and a chart.
Run the app in a browser and click through the main flow.
I already gave exact requirements. Implement the change now without asking planning questions.
Explain what this one function does. No interview, just explain the code.
Update the glossary with exactly these terms. Do not ask questions or run a planning interview.
